niche: Practical AI for indie builders and self-hosters
audience: Engineers, hackers, and creators who care about local-first AI, agentic workflows, and shipping real demos on commodity hardware (e.g. Raspberry Pi, mini PCs, single-GPU rigs).
tone: Skeptical, hands-on, slightly opinionated. No hype. Prefer concrete demos over abstract claims. Concise, technical, and direct.
perspective: First-person from a builder who actually tests things on a Raspberry Pi 4 and is allergic to vapor announcements.
signature_angles: Does it actually run on a Pi 4?
Replace a paid SaaS with this open-source thing

examples.input_title: Llamafile 0.9 ships single-binary distribution for tiny LLMs
examples.input_description: Mozilla's Llamafile project releases a single binary that bundles model weights + runtime, runnable on CPU-only hardware including Raspberry Pi.

pack.hook: One file. No GPU. Llama running on a Pi 4 in under five minutes.
pack.intro_context: Llamafile collapses model + runtime into a single executable. The 0.9 cut adds ARM64 support and quietly raises the bar for self-hosters.
three_key_points: Single binary means no Python, no CUDA, no setup hell.
ARM64 build runs a 3B model on a Pi 4 at ~6 tok/s without quantization tricks.
Trade-off: no streaming server on Day 1, you wire your own.
three_beat_structure: Hook: One file beats six pages of setup notes.
Payoff: Live demo from `wget` to first token on a Pi 4.
CTA: Try it on whatever box is closest to a power outlet.
pack.demo_segment: SSH into the Pi, wget the .llamafile, chmod +x, run, hit the local port. Show real tokens per second on a stopwatch.
pack.caveats: Cold start is slow. Memory pressure climbs above 3.7GB during context fill. ARM build still misses a few quantization paths.
pack.closing_takeaway: If you only test one local-LLM project this week, this is the one.
pack.call_to_action: Run it on whatever Pi you already own and post your tok/s.
pack.short_script: Llamafile 0.9 just dropped and it actually runs on my Pi 4. One binary. No CUDA. I clocked six tokens a second on a 3B model and the setup took four minutes. The catch is memory pressure climbs over 3.7 gigs once context fills. Worth testing tonight.
pack.visual_idea: Stopwatch overlay on the SSH session as the model loads.

suggested_titles.curiosity: The single-file LLM that finally runs on a Pi 4
suggested_titles.practical: Llamafile 0.9: a real local LLM in four minutes flat
suggested_titles.contrarian: You do not need a GPU to self-host a useful LLM
suggested_titles.tutorial: How to run Llamafile 0.9 on a Raspberry Pi 4 today

broll_list: Top-down shot of the Pi 4 board with the SSH terminal overlaid
Stopwatch animation timing the cold start
tok/s graph filling in live during a prompt
pack.insight: Distribution beats raw quality for self-hosters. A binary you can scp is worth more than a slightly smarter model behind a paywall.
hooks: Local LLMs lost the binary war until last week.
One file. No CUDA. Real tokens on a Pi 4.
If self-hosting is dead, why did this just go viral?
